DARRYL DUNCAN, President Chief Executive Officer Monongalia Health System, Morgantown,
Duncan served this capacity since January 2011.
Health System parent company General Hospital, Preston Memorial Hospital, Monongalia EMS, HealthCare Equipment Supplies Village Heritage Point.
Before being named President CEO, Duncan served Chief Operating Officer General Hospital from 2006-2011.
Prior joining Health System, Duncan been Chief Operating Officer Provena Saint Joseph Hospital, 251- hospital Elgin, Illinois from 2004 2006.
Duncan more than years' experience either Chief Operating Officer Chief Executive Officer both for-profit not-for-profit hospitals.
Duncan received Bachelor Science Masters Science degrees Industrial Engineering from West Virginia University Masters Hospital Health Administration degree from Xavier University, Cincinnati, Ohio.

Researchers from Karolinska Institutet Kancera have developed molecule that makes cancer cells sensitive radiotherapy.
study published Nature Communications, researchers describe block cancer cells' ability repair their thus stop survival cancer cells.
Radiotherapy most common cancer treatments.
damages DNA, causing cancer cells stop growing damage left unrepaired.
Unfortunately, some cancers resistant radiotherapy treatment also damages healthy cells, thus limiting amount radiation that used with respect normal tissue damage.
collaboration with Swedish company Kancera researchers Karolinska Institutet have discovered mechanism that cancer cells repair damage.
current study, researchers discovered that cancer cells protein called PFKFB3 repair damage that occurs radiation therapy.
They found that protein locates sites damage cell nucleus where protein regulates cancer cell's ability repair thus survive.
research groups Kancera Karolinska Institutet have developed drug candidate which blocks protein ability repair damage.
They demonstrated that cancer cells treated with drug survive upon radiotherapy laboratory experiments.
known that levels PFKFB3 much higher cancer cells than healthy cells.
However, discovery that PFKFB3 regulates repair upon radiotherapy very exciting," says Nina Gustafsson, Assistant Professor Team Leader Translational Medicine Department Oncology-Pathology, Karolinska Institutet, study together with Professor Thomas Helleday same department.
Since normal, healthy cells dependent PFKFB3 proper repair, researchers hope that combination therapy with radiation chemotherapy will well tolerated.
goal further develop drug foundation cancer treatment with less side effects than treatments currently available.
study researchers Karolinska Institutet, operating SciLifeLab, Kancera collaboration with researchers from Stockholm University, University Sheffield, Sprint Bioscience, Roma University, Emory University School Medicine more.
research been funded Swedish Society Medical Research, European Union's Horizon 2020 research innovation program, together with Torsten Söderberg Ragnar Söderberg Foundation more.
"Targeting PFKFB3 radiosensitizes cancer cells suppresses homologous recombination".

Gustafsson, Katarina Färnegårdh, Nadilly Bonagas, Anna Huguet Ninou, Petra Groth, Elisee Wiita, Mattias Jönsson, Kenth Hallberg, Jemina Lehto, Rosa Pennisi, Jessica Martinsson, Carina Norström, Jessica Hollers, Johan Schultz, Martin Andersson, Natalia Markova, Petra Marttila, Baek Kim, Martin Norin, Thomas Olin, Thomas Helleday.
